journal.title: New projects
journal.subject: My methods to reducing burnouts and unmaintained projects.
journal.body:
I've been working on my argument parsing library for c *plib* for the past
year and a bit and I've just had the realization that my best work is done
through mass iteration and when the project is spanned over a long amount
of time. Over my spare time I've tried writing programs in similar detail 
to plib but I always come to a stop road where I've either done to much 
work and I'm now burnt out or I've realized that the project is out of my
skill-level or constraints. 

Because of this I've decided that while I'm attending school I will do 
yearly projects in a similar style to plib in the hopes that these 
larger projects force me to slow down and therefore actually finish the
project.

Now that plib is DONE for the forseeable future I can focus on another
side project. I think its fair to make a project a year seeing as it
usualy is my best work and of course I have school and all that.
